key_insight: NuDEAL unifies three GPU-accelerated deterministic transport solvers on unstructured meshes, letting users trade off accuracy, speed, and memory for advanced reactor analysis.
engineering_relevance: The framework enables practical whole-core neutronic simulations for complex reactor geometries on GPUs, reducing reliance on large CPU clusters.
method: MOC/HFEM: planar method of characteristics coupled with hybrid finite elements
DGMOC: discontinuous Galerkin method of characteristics
DFEM-SN: discontinuous finite element discrete ordinates solver
evidence_signal: The solvers were verified on the C5G7 benchmark and applied to ABTR, Empire microreactor, and MSRE cases; DFEM-SN reported eigenvalue errors below 50 pcm, while the other methods showed strong efficiency.
watch_item: Performance depends on GPU-specific implementation details such as memory alignment, compressed-flux storage, and sweep ordering, which may affect portability and scaling.
paper_title: Verification and Performance Assessment of NuDEAL, a GPU-Accelerated Deterministic Transport Framework on Unstructured Meshes
